description: Deduplicate the architecture illustration, replace all ASCII diagrams with SVG illustrations, and clean up the shared illustration component pattern.
qualityChecks: cd docs && bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip

userStories.title: Move ArchitectureIllustration to shared illustrations.tsx and reuse on architecture.mdx
userStories.description: As a docs reader, I want the architecture page to show the same polished SVG as the homepage so that the visual language is consistent and the concept is represented by a single illustration.
acceptanceCriteria: The `ArchitectureIllustration` component is exported from `docs/components/illustrations.tsx` (not defined locally in `docs/app/(marketing)/page.tsx`)
The homepage (`docs/app/(marketing)/page.tsx`) imports `ArchitectureIllustration` from `docs/components/illustrations.tsx` and renders it exactly where it was before
The architecture page (`docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx`) uses `<ArchitectureIllustration />` instead of the ASCII art diagram (lines 9-25)
The ASCII art code block in architecture.mdx (lines 9-25) is completely removed
`ArchitectureIllustration` is registered in `docs/components/MDX.tsx`'s `docComponents` object
The SVG marker IDs in ArchitectureIllustration are prefixed (e.g. `arch-arrow-right`, `arch-arrow-left`) to avoid collisions with other illustrations
The homepage renders identically to before (the outer wrapper div with shadow and border in page.tsx is preserved — only the component import changes)
`cd docs && bun run build` passes
`cd docs && bun run type-check` passes
`cd docs && bun run lint` passes
`cd docs && bun run knip` passes
userStories.notes: The `ArchitectureIllustration` component is currently defined as a local component at docs/app/(marketing)/page.tsx:27-494. It is a ~460-line inline SVG with viewBox 0 0 880 320 showing three neo-brutalist boxes (AI Agent, OpenTabs Server, Your Browser) connected by arrows labeled MCP and WebSocket. Move it to docs/components/illustrations.tsx alongside QuickStartFlow and PluginStructure. Then import it in page.tsx and in MDX.tsx. In architecture.mdx, replace the ASCII code block (lines 9-25, between the intro paragraph and the ## MCP Server heading) with `<ArchitectureIllustration />`. The marker IDs are currently `arrow-right` and `arrow-left` — rename to `arch-arrow-right` and `arch-arrow-left` to avoid collisions. Update all `markerEnd` and the marker `id` attributes. The component wrapping on the homepage is a separate concern — the homepage uses an outer div with shadow effect (page.tsx:528-533), so only the component definition moves, not the wrapper.

userStories.title: Replace config directory tree ASCII art with SVG illustration
userStories.description: As a docs reader, I want the Configuration reference page's directory tree to be a polished SVG illustration so it matches the visual quality of other docs illustrations.
acceptanceCriteria: A new `ConfigDirectory` component is exported from `docs/components/illustrations.tsx`

It shows the `~/.opentabs/` directory structure with: `config.json`, `auth.json`, `audit.log`, `server.log`, and `extension/` subdirectory containing `manifest.json`, `background.js`, `adapters/`, `.opentabs-version`
The illustration uses the terminal-window style (traffic lights in header, like `PluginStructure`) with the title `~/.opentabs/`
File entries have muted descriptions matching the current ASCII art labels
The `extension/` directory is visually distinguished (e.g. primary-colored directory name like `src/` in PluginStructure)
The ASCII code block in `docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx` (lines 312-323) is replaced with `<ConfigDirectory />`
`ConfigDirectory` is registered in `docs/components/MDX.tsx`
The illustration follows all neo-brutalist style rules (CSS variables, 3px borders, drop shadow, no border-radius, var(--font-mono))
`cd docs && bun run build` passes
`cd docs && bun run type-check` passes
`cd docs && bun run lint` passes
`cd docs && bun run knip` passes
userStories.notes: The ASCII diagram is at docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx:312-323. It is a simple tree structure. Model the SVG after the existing `PluginStructure` component (docs/components/illustrations.tsx:195-372) which uses the terminal-window pattern with traffic lights. Use a viewBox that fits the content (approximately 520x280) and `max-w-lg` for the className. Files should be listed as text entries with muted descriptions. The `extension/` directory and its children should be indented. Use the same spacing and font sizes as PluginStructure. Marker IDs are not needed (no arrows). The section heading '## Config directory structure' should remain above the illustration.

userStories.title: Replace monorepo project structure ASCII art with SVG illustration
userStories.description: As a docs reader, I want the Dev Setup page's project structure to be a polished SVG illustration that matches the terminal-window style of other docs illustrations.
acceptanceCriteria: A new `MonorepoStructure` component is exported from `docs/components/illustrations.tsx`
It shows the top-level monorepo layout: `platform/` (with mcp-server, browser-extension, plugin-sdk, plugin-tools, cli, shared, create-plugin), `plugins/` (with examples), `e2e/`, `scripts/`, `docs/`
Uses the terminal-window style with traffic lights and title `opentabs/`
Directory names like `platform/`, `plugins/`, `e2e/`, `scripts/`, `docs/` are highlighted in primary color as directory headings
Sub-entries have muted descriptions matching the current ASCII labels
The ASCII code block in `docs/content/docs/contributing/dev-setup.mdx` (lines 180-196) is replaced with `<MonorepoStructure />`
`MonorepoStructure` is registered in `docs/components/MDX.tsx`
`cd docs && bun run build` passes
`cd docs && bun run type-check` passes
`cd docs && bun run lint` passes
`cd docs && bun run knip` passes
userStories.notes: The ASCII diagram is at docs/content/docs/contributing/dev-setup.mdx:180-196. It is a two-level tree. Model after `PluginStructure` and `ConfigDirectory` (from US-002). The viewBox should be larger to fit more entries (approximately 560x400). Use `max-w-lg`. The `platform/` section is the largest with 7 sub-entries. Keep descriptions concise (e.g., 'MCP server', 'Chrome extension (MV3)', 'Plugin authoring SDK'). The section heading '## Project Structure' remains above.

userStories.title: Replace dispatch flow ASCII line with SVG illustration in resources-prompts.mdx
userStories.description: As a docs reader, I want the Resources & Prompts page's dispatch flow to be a proper SVG illustration instead of a plain-text arrow chain.
acceptanceCriteria: A new `DispatchFlow` component is exported from `docs/components/illustrations.tsx`
It shows a horizontal flow: AI Agent -> MCP Server -> Chrome Extension -> Adapter IIFE -> Page Context, with arrows between each step
Each step is a labeled box in the neo-brutalist style
The flow uses a compact horizontal layout (similar to QuickStartFlow but with 5 steps instead of 3)
The ASCII code block in `docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx` (lines 314-315) is replaced with `<DispatchFlow />`
`DispatchFlow` is registered in `docs/components/MDX.tsx`
`cd docs && bun run build` passes
`cd docs && bun run type-check` passes
`cd docs && bun run lint` passes
`cd docs && bun run knip` passes
userStories.notes: The ASCII diagram is at docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx:314-315. It is a single line: `AI Agent → MCP Server → Chrome Extension → Adapter IIFE → Page Context`. Create a compact horizontal flow similar to QuickStartFlow (docs/components/illustrations.tsx:16-189) but with 5 narrower boxes. Use a wider viewBox (approximately 900x100) with `w-full`. Each box should have just a label (no header bar needed — keep it minimal since the boxes are small). Use `max-w-3xl` to prevent it from being too wide. Arrow marker IDs should be prefixed as `df-arrow`. The section heading and explanatory text around it should be preserved.

userStories.title: Replace audit output ASCII table with styled presentation in logging-debugging.mdx
userStories.description: As a docs reader, I want the Logging & Debugging page's audit output example to be either a proper SVG illustration or a well-styled code block that doesn't use ASCII table-drawing characters.
acceptanceCriteria: The ASCII table in `docs/content/docs/guides/logging-debugging.mdx` (lines 140-146) is replaced
The replacement shows the same information (Time, Tool, OK status, Duration) in a visually clear format
If using an SVG: follows all neo-brutalist style rules and is registered in MDX.tsx
If using a styled code block: uses a clean format without ASCII table-drawing characters (plain text with clear alignment is acceptable for CLI output examples)
`cd docs && bun run build` passes
`cd docs && bun run type-check` passes
`cd docs && bun run lint` passes
`cd docs && bun run knip` passes
userStories.notes: The ASCII table is at docs/content/docs/guides/logging-debugging.mdx:140-146. This is a CLI output example showing the `opentabs audit` command result. Since this is showing CLI output (not a conceptual diagram), a styled code block may be more appropriate than an SVG. The current issue is the `─────────────────` line separator. A clean approach: use a code block with `bash` or plain text language hint, replace the horizontal rule with spaces or a simple separator. Alternatively, create a small SVG illustration styled as a terminal window showing the audit output. Either approach is acceptable — choose whichever produces a cleaner result. The key constraint is: no raw ASCII box-drawing characters in the documentation.
